h3: Citizens and permanent residents of the United States
Passport holders of certain countries or territories

sections.heading: Visitor visa
items: You need a visitor visa if you’re from a visa-required country and you plan to:
You also need a visitor visa if you’re from a visa-required country and you’re crossing the border by:
Apply for a visitor visa
bullets: visit Canada (even if you’re travelling by air and it’s for less than 48 hours)
stay in Canada more than 48 hours while transiting

sections.heading: Transit visa
items: You need a transit visa if you’re from a visa-required country and:
Apply for a transit visa
bullets: your international flight stops at a Canadian airport on its way to another country
you’ll be connecting between 2 international flights at one or more Canadian airports
your transit through Canada will last 48 hours or less
you don’t have a valid visitor visa
you’re not eligible for an eTA .

sections.heading: eTA
items: You need an eTA to transit through Canada by air if you’re from an eTA-required country .
You don’t need an eTA if you’re transiting through Canada and you enter by train, bus, boat or cruise ship. You do need to bring the right travel documents .
Apply for an eTA

sections.heading: Citizens and permanent residents of the United States
items: US citizens and lawful permanent residents of the U.S. can transit through Canada without a visa by:
You just need to bring the right travel documents .

sections.heading: Passport holders of certain countries or territories
items: You may be able to transit without a visa if you hold a passport from one of the following countries or territories and you meet certain conditions:
Who can transit without a visa
bullets: Taiwan (holder of passport without an personal identification number)
